reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku (not Tokyo). Tool only fetched Akita and returns generic spots (stone circles, ravines, lakes, museums) without sakura tagging. Akita is in Tohoku so geographic constraint is met, but sakura intent not surfaced. Data is real and well-structured.
missing_data: Sakura-specific tagging/filtering; coverage of other Tohoku prefectures (Aomori Hirosaki, Akita Kakunodate, Fukushima Miharu).
improvement_hint: Add sakura/season tagging to spots and support multi-prefecture region queries (region=Tohoku).

reasoning: User wants major Niigata sake breweries; tool returns MAFF GI items (edamame, snow carrots, lotus root) — no sake. Data accurate but wrong category for query. Niigata's famous breweries (Hakkaisan, Asahi-Shuzo/Kubota, Kakurei) absent.
missing_data: Sake brewery entries (Hakkaisan, Asahi-Shuzo, Imayotsukasa, Kakurei, Hokusetsu, etc.).
improvement_hint: Add a sake/brewery category sourcing from National Tax Agency GI sake list and Niigata Sake Brewers Association.

reasoning: Hakuba ski+onsen query. Top results are nav-chrome items ('観光課', '景観・環境・観光') from municipal scrape with quality_score 0.7-0.75 ranked above real attractions. No ski resort or onsen tagging surfaced. Real spots (lakes, museum, alpine garden) included but ski/onsen pairing absent.
missing_data: Hakuba ski resorts (Happo-One, Goryu, Iwatake, Hakuba 47, Tsugaike, Cortina) and onsen (Hakuba Happo, Mimizuku, Obinata).
wrong_data: Municipal nav pages '観光課'/'景観・環境・観光' shouldn't be 'must_see' tier.
improvement_hint: Filter nav-chrome from municipal scrapes; add ski_resort and onsen kinds; surface them on combined queries.

reasoning: Kyoto-only fetch for Kansai autumn foliage; returns major must_see temples/castles (Nijo, Toji, Nishi-Honganji, Ninna-ji) with rich metadata but no autumn-foliage filtering and no other Kansai prefectures (Osaka, Nara, Hyogo, Wakayama, Shiga).
missing_data: Foliage tagging (Tofukuji, Eikando, Kiyomizu); Shiga (Hieizan, Eigenji), Nara (Yoshino, Tatsuta), Osaka (Mino).
improvement_hint: Add seasonal_highlights field (sakura/koyo/lavender) and region-level multi-prefecture aggregation.

reasoning: Arabic query for Kansai sakura spots; Nara fetch returns generic shrines/temples with name field='神社・仏閣' (category label, not actual asset name) for top results. Underlying URLs lead to real assets but presentation is unusable. No sakura tagging. Nara's iconic Yoshino sakura missing.
missing_data: Yoshino-yama, Nara-koen sakura spots; multi-prefecture Kansai coverage; multilingual name surfacing.
wrong_data: Spot 'name' field returns '神社・仏閣' rather than the actual asset name in municipal_scrape items.
improvement_hint: Fix municipal scrape parser to extract actual asset name; add sakura kind/tag.

reasoning: User asks for stargazing on Okinawa remote islands. Response gives Naha-area castles/monuments (Tamaudun, Shuri-jo, Nakagusuku, Sefa-utaki) — none are remote islands or stargazing spots. Iriomote, Hateruma, Ishigaki dark-sky locations absent.
missing_data: Yaeyama islands (Hateruma=southernmost dark sky, Ishigaki Iriomote dark sky park), Miyako, Aka, Kerama.
improvement_hint: Add municipality filter for Yaeyama/Miyako and dark_sky/stargazing kind.

reasoning: Thai query asks for rural onsen ryokan in Hokkaido. Top results are urban Sapporo/Kushiro business hotels (ANA Holiday Inn, ANA Crowne Plaza) and apartments. No 'onsen_ryokan' filter applied; no rural location filter (Noboribetsu, Tokachi, Niseko countryside, Kawayu).
missing_data: Onsen ryokan in Noboribetsu, Sounkyo, Akan, Tokachigawa, Niseko Hirafu/Yumoto, Yunokawa.
improvement_hint: Support lodging_type filter (onsen_ryokan) and rural-vs-urban classification.

reasoning: User wants Seto Inland Sea islands. Kagawa-only fetch gives mainland castles and Ritsurin Garden, lakes — no islands surfaced (Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Megijima are absent in shown excerpt). Mannō Lake/Ritsurin not Seto islands.
missing_data: Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima, Inujima, Megijima, Ogijima island entries with island=true tag; multi-prefecture Setouchi (Okayama, Hiroshima, Ehime).
improvement_hint: Add island/archipelago kind and Setouchi region filter.

reasoning: User wants indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. METI craft list gives 'Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave' (related but a textile, not a workshop) plus pottery and washi (Awa Indigo-dyed washi mentioned). No actual workshop venues (Aizumi Historical Museum, Buaisou) listed. No workshop-experience data.
missing_data: Workshop venues: Aizumi Town Historical Museum of Indigo, Buaisou (神山町), Aiya, indigo dyeing experience studios with addresses.
improvement_hint: Cross-reference craft designation with venues offering hands-on experience (taikan/workshop kind).

reasoning: German query for Koyasan shukubo. Returns Wakayama hotels (HOTEL KII TANABE, Marriott, business hotels in Kushimoto) — no Koya-san temples/shukubo. Mt. Koya has 50+ shukubo (Ekoin, Henjoson-in, Shojoshin-in) but none surfaced.

improvement_hint: Add shukubo lodging_type and municipality filter (高野町); cross-reference with Koyasan Shukubo Association.

reasoning: search_area for 武家屋敷 returns relevant samurai districts: Buke Yashiki (Shimane Matsue), Old Uchiyama (Fukui Ōno), Chiran (Kagoshima, with Important Preservation District designation), Aizu Bukeyashiki (Fukushima). All outside Kyoto, all real, well-tagged. Strong response.
missing_data: Kakunodate (Akita), Hagi (Yamaguchi), Kanazawa Nagamachi could be surfaced higher.
improvement_hint: Boost ranking for Important Preservation District designations to surface most-known spots first.

reasoning: Indonesian query for Akita summer festivals. Returns 3 intangible cultural property festivals (Dainichido Bugaku=Jan, Kakunodate=Sep, Tsuchizaki Shinmei=Jul). Tsuchizaki is summer; Kakunodate is autumn; Dainichido is January. Akita's iconic Kanto Matsuri (Aug 3-6) absent. Indonesian translation provided.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Matsuri, Hanawa Bayashi, Omagari Hanabi (national fireworks competition).
improvement_hint: Add summer-season filter and major regional summer matsuri (Kanto, Tanabata) data, not just intangible heritage.

reasoning: Korean query for small port towns in San'in. Shimane fetch returns Iwami Ginzan (mine), Matsue Castle, Gassantoda Castle — none are port towns. Mihonoseki, Hamada-ko, Sakaiminato (Tottori though) are missing.
missing_data: Mihonoseki, Yunotsu (port heritage), Hamada port, Mitsu (Iwami coast); cross-reference with Tottori for full San'in coverage.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_port/port_town kind; expand to multi-prefecture San'in (Shimane+Tottori+northern Yamaguchi).

reasoning: User asks for washi paper-making experience in Gifu. Response includes Mino Ware (pottery), Hida Shunkei lacquer, Nagiso Lathe, Ichii carving — but Gifu's most famous craft (Mino Washi) is conspicuously not in the truncated portion shown, though it should be category-craft #0903 or similar. Workshop venue (Mino Washi no Sato) not surfaced.
missing_data: Mino Washi (本美濃紙) entry; Mino Washi no Sato Kaikan workshop venue with address.
improvement_hint: Cross-reference craft with venue offering taiken (hands-on); ensure Mino Washi appears for Gifu+washi queries.

reasoning: Spanish query for Noto fishing villages. Returns Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, National Crafts Museum, Nanao Castle — Kanazawa is Kaga not Noto, only Nanao is Noto-adjacent. No Wajima/Suzu/Notoro fishing villages. Notamap of fishing port towns shown.
missing_data: Wajima (asaichi morning market), Sosogi, Suzu, Notojima, Noto-cho fishing villages; coastal port settlements.
improvement_hint: Add Noto Peninsula sub-region filter and fishing_village/port kind.

reasoning: Search 砂丘 (sand dunes) returns Byōdō-in Temple via 'heritage_class_match' as top result — completely wrong. Match algorithm misfires. Query mentions Tottori excluded but other dunes (Nakatajima, Sarukura, Niigata Sasagawa) not surfaced in shown excerpt.
missing_data: Nakatajima Dunes (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka), Sarukura (Aomori), Niigata Sasagawa Sand Dunes, Yumigahama (Tottori but smaller).
wrong_data: Top result Byōdō-in Temple via heritage_class_match — irrelevant to sand dunes query.
improvement_hint: Fix heritage_class_match scoring; require kind/keyword overlap before falling back to heritage class.

reasoning: Chinese query for San'in traditional crafts (Shimane). Returns 4 METI-designated crafts: Iwami Yaki, Sekishu Washi, Unshu Soroban, Izumo Stone Lanterns — all genuine San'in/Shimane crafts with bilingual descriptions and source URLs. Strong, on-target response.
missing_data: Tottori crafts (Inshu Washi, Yumigahama Gasuri) for full San'in coverage.

reasoning: Query about Hokkaido lavender fields beyond Furano. Returns Matsumae Castle, Goryōkaku, Showa-shinzan, Sapporo Clock Tower — no lavender fields. Famous Nakafurano, Biei Shikisai-no-Oka, Hokuryu absent.

reasoning: Filipino query (interpreting 'kape' as 'old/古い') for traditional towns in Kyushu. Oita-only fetch returns Usa Jingu, Hells of Beppu, Oka Castle, Beppu Tower — none are old townscapes. Yufuin and Hita Mameda historic district missing. Generic '九重町観光協会' nav-chrome included.

reasoning: Query for kominka (traditional house) lodging in Hokuriku. Ishikawa fetch returns 'Student housing 1', AB Hotel, ANA Crowne Plaza, generic guest house — no kominka. Wajima/Noto kominka stays absent. Some entries have null name — poor quality.

reasoning: Query for mountain shrines on Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage returns Wakanoura (scenic, not mountain shrines per se) and Saigoku 33 pilgrimage. Kumano Sanzan, Koyasan, Yoshino-Omine all relevant but not surfaced in shown excerpt. Returns rich heritage stories but doesn't directly equip agent with mountain shrine list.

reasoning: Query for summer fireworks along Sea of Japan coast. Niigata fetch returns 129 festivals but top result is 'Uonuma Shibazakura Matsuri' (May, moss phlox, not fireworks). Nagaoka Hanabi (a top-3 Japan fireworks) likely buried. No fireworks-tag filter applied.

reasoning: Beppu Bamboo Crafts (METI #0630) returned with full description, designation date, production area — exactly answers query. Hakata Ori also listed (covers Oita partially). Strong, accurate, useful.

reasoning: Query for cycling routes in rural Shikoku. Ehime fetch returns Uwajima Castle, Matsuyama Castle, Ozu Castle, Uchiko-za — no cycling content. Shimanami Kaido (world-famous cycling route ending in Imabari, Ehime) is conspicuously missing.
